sections.title: Top-level finding: there is no unit-test framework
sections.content:
`npm test` runs `scripts/validate-content.mjs`, which only lints the JSON/Markdown content files (structure, word counts, cross-references). **Zero application logic in `src/` has a test around it** ‚Äî no Vitest/Jest, no `*.test.ts` files, no component tests. Every gap below assumes you add Vitest (pairs naturally with the existing Vite setup):

```bash
npm i -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom jsdom
```
```json
// package.json ‚Äî add
"scripts": { "test:unit": "vitest run" }
```

---

sections.title: `src/lib/content.ts` ‚Äî highest priority, all pure logic, zero coverage
`isBook` (implicit, not exported) ‚Äî validation gate for every book.json
sections.content:
Gaps: missing `map.chapters` array, empty chapters array, chapter missing `number`/`title`/`summary`, non-object input, `null`/`undefined` input.

```ts
// src/lib/content.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
// isBook isn't exported ‚Äî either export it for testing, or test indirectly via `books`
```
This is the biggest structural gap: **`isBook` isn't exported**, so it can't be unit tested in isolation today. Recommend exporting it (it's a pure predicate) so validity rules can be tested directly instead of only through the glob-loaded `books` array.

sections.title: `countWords`
sections.content:
Untested edge cases: empty string, whitespace-only string, single word, multiple internal spaces/tabs/newlines.

```ts
import { countWords } from './content'

describe('countWords', () => {
  it('returns 0 for empty string', () => expect(countWords('')).toBe(0))
  it('returns 0 for whitespace-only string', () => expect(countWords('   \n\t ')).toBe(0))
  it('counts single word', () => expect(countWords('hello')).toBe(1))
  it('collapses multiple separators', () => expect(countWords('a  b\nc\td')).toBe(4))
})
```

sections.title: `loadChapter` ‚Äî the most complex function in the module, fully untested
`bookStats`
sections.content:
Untested: `savedPct` when `totalMinutes > originalMinutes` (should clamp to 0 via `Math.max(0, ‚Ä¶)` ‚Äî verify it does, since a book with very few original pages but many chapters could invert this), the `Math.max(4, ‚Ä¶)` floor on `mapMinutes`, the `c.minutes || 9` fallback when a map chapter has `minutes: 0` (note: `0 || 9` ‚Üí `9`, which is probably *not* intended if 0 is ever a legitimate value ‚Äî worth a test to pin the behavior either way).

```ts
import { bookStats } from './content'

describe('bookStats', () => {
  it('clamps savedPct to 0 when distilled time exceeds original', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
  it('falls back to 9 minutes per chapter when minutes is 0 or missing', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
  it('enforces 4-minute floor on mapMinutes for tiny intros', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
})
```

sections.title: `searchBooks`
sections.content:
Untested: empty/whitespace query returns all books unsorted-by-score, multi-term queries (AND-like scoring across terms), case-insensitivity, matches only in `weak` tier (chapter titles) still surface the book, no matches returns `[]`, scoring tie-breaking (stable sort not guaranteed by spec ‚Äî worth pinning behavior).

```ts
import { searchBooks } from './content'

describe('searchBooks', () => {
  it('returns all books for an empty query', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
  it('is case-insensitive', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
  it('ranks title/author matches above chapter-title-only matches', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
  it('returns empty array when nothing matches', () => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ })
})
```

sections.title: `fmtDuration`
`getBook`, `booksInCategory`, `categoryOf`, `hasChapterFile`
sections.content:
Untested: `getBook(undefined)` ‚Üí `undefined`, unknown id ‚Üí `undefined`, `booksInCategory` with a category that has zero books ‚Üí `[]`, `categoryOf` for a book whose `categoryId` doesn't match any category ‚Üí `undefined`.

---

sections.title: `src/lib/progress.ts` ‚Äî has an internal `sanitize` function that's the real risk surface
`src/lib/store.ts` ‚Äî the localStorage abstraction, currently has no failure-mode tests
`src/lib/markdown.tsx` ‚Äî no XSS/sanitization test, and no test at all
Integration test gaps (pages/components ‚Äî currently 0 coverage of any kind)
sections.content:
No test touches `App.tsx`, `Library.tsx`, `BookMap.tsx`, `Reader.tsx`, `Cover.tsx`, or `NavBar.tsx`. Highest-value integration scenarios, in priority order:

1. **Routing** (`App.tsx`) ‚Äî navigating to an unknown `:bookId` should not crash; `getBook` returning `undefined` needs a handled UI path (redirect/404) in `BookMap`/`Reader` ‚Äî worth confirming this is actually handled, since `content.ts` returns `undefined` rather than throwing.
2. **Library search** ‚Äî typing into the search box filters via `searchBooks`, clearing it restores the full list.
3. **Reader progress flow** ‚Äî opening a chapter marks it read (`markChapterRead`), progress bar reflects `completionPct`, and this survives a simulated reload (localStorage round-trip via `store.ts`).
4. **Prefs** ‚Äî toggling `appTheme`/`readerTheme`/`fontScale` updates DOM classes/styles, and `bumpFontScale` clamps at the documented `0.85`‚Äì`1.3` bounds (currently untested even as a unit: `bumpFontScale(999)` should clamp to `1.3`, `bumpFontScale(-999)` to `0.85`).

```ts
import { bumpFontScale } from './prefs'
// this one is a pure-logic gap hiding in a UI-adjacent file ‚Äî cheap to close first
describe('bumpFontScale', () => {
  it('clamps to 1.3 max', () => { /* seed store at 1.29, bump by 1, expect 1.3 */ })
  it('clamps to 0.85 min', () => { /* seed store at 0.86, bump by -1, expect 0.85 */ })
})
```

---

sections.title: Summary priority order
sections.content:
1. Set up Vitest (currently `npm test` tests content files, not code).
2. Export `isBook` from `content.ts` and `sanitize` from `progress.ts` ‚Äî both are load-bearing defensive logic with no seam to test them through currently.
3. Extract chapter-markdown parsing out of `loadChapter` into a pure, glob-free function so it's testable without fixture files.
4. Cover `store.ts` failure modes (corrupt JSON, quota errors) ‚Äî it's the one module every persisted-state bug would trace back to.
5. Add `bumpFontScale`/`completionPct`/`fmtDuration` boundary tests ‚Äî cheap, pure, currently zero coverage.
6. Integration tests for Library search and Reader progress-tracking round-trip.

// progress.test.ts ‚Äî note: sanitize is not exported; recommend exporting it,
// same rationale as isBook ‚Äî it's the actual defensive logic worth pinning.

rawOutputPreview:
## Top-level finding: there is no unit-test framework

`npm test` runs `scripts/validate-content.mjs`, which only lints the JSON/Markdown content files (structure, word counts, cross-references). **Zero application logic in `src/` has a test around it** ‚Äî no Vitest/Jest, no `*.test.ts` files, no component tests. Every gap below assumes you add Vitest (pairs naturally with the existing Vite setup):

```bash
npm i -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom jsdom
```
```json
// package.json ‚Äî add
"scripts": { "test:unit": "vitest run" }
```

---

## `src/lib/content.ts` ‚Äî highest priority, all pure logic, zero coverage

### `isBook` (implicit, not exported) ‚Äî validation gate for every book.json
Gaps: missing `map.chapters` array, empty chapters array, chapter missing `number`/`title`/`summary`, non-object input, `null`/`undefined` input.

```ts
// src/lib/content.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
// isBook isn't exported ‚Äî either export it for testing, or test indirectly via `books`
```
This is the biggest structural gap: **`isBook` isn't exported**, so it can't be unit tested in isolation today. Recommend exporting it (it's a pure predicate) so validity rules can be tested directly instead of only through the glob-loaded `books` array.

### `countWords`
Untested edge cases: empty string, whitespace-only string, single word, multiple internal spaces/tabs/newlines.

```ts
import { countWords } from './content'

describe('countWords', () => {
  it('returns 0 for empty string', () => expect(countWords('')).toBe(0))
  it('returns 0 for whitespace-only string', () => expect(countWords('   \n\t ')).toBe(0))
  it('counts single word', () => expect(countWords('hello')).toBe(1))
  it('collapses multiple separators', () => expect(countWords('a  b\nc\td')).toBe(4))
})
```

### `loadChapter` ‚Äî the most complex function in the module, fully untested
Gaps:
- Non-existent `bookId`/chapter number ‚Üí should return `null`.
- Chapter file with no `# Tit
